title: Anthropic opens early talks with Samsung to build a custom 2nm AI chip
subtitle: The exploratory conversations follow OpenAI's Broadcom-built Jalapeño and mark Anthropic's first concrete step toward silicon independence from Nvidia.
author: AESOP AI Engine
body: Anthropic is in early talks with Samsung to manufacture its first custom AI chip on Samsung's 2-nanometer process, The Information reported on July 2 and Bloomberg confirmed the next day. The discussions are exploratory — Anthropic has not decided what the chip will be optimized for, no prototypes exist, and there is no manufacturing timeline. The company recently hired Clive Chan, an early member of OpenAI's silicon team, to help lead the effort. Samsung participated as a strategic investor alongside SK Hynix and Micron in Anthropic's most recent fundraise.

The technical detail worth noting is the node choice. Samsung Foundry's 2nm process is a direct challenge to TSMC's N2, which every current-generation frontier accelerator depends on. If Anthropic wants both an alternative to Nvidia and an alternative to TSMC in a single move, Samsung is the only vendor that offers both. That combination is what makes the talks strategically interesting even at the exploratory stage.

The move follows OpenAI's Jalapeño announcement last week — a Broadcom-built inference chip OpenAI claims delivers better performance-per-watt than incumbents — and lands in a year when every frontier lab has stopped treating custom silicon as optional. Google has TPU v8, Amazon has Trainium 3, Microsoft has Maia 200 in talks with Anthropic itself. The pattern is consistent: labs are trying to convert their compute buyer power into hardware control before the next scaling wave locks their unit economics to a single supplier.

A takeaway for learners: 'AI competition' is not just about model quality anymore — it is increasingly a fight over manufacturing capacity, foundry access, and packaging technology. If you are studying computer science, spending a few weeks understanding how a modern accelerator is designed, packaged, and yielded is one of the highest-leverage things you can do. The next decade of AI hiring will pull heavily from anyone who can bridge model research and hardware.

sources.title: Anthropic is discussing a new custom chip with Samsung — TechCrunch
